title: phanera macrostachya
intro: Phanera macrostachya, also known as the Indian cordia, is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, which are derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The health benefits of Phanera macrostachya include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a cardiotonic and diuretic. Phanera macrostachya can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
